source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of mesenchymal progenitor cells in adipose tissue
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal fibroblast/adipocyte progenitor populations
CD34 marks adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells and is used for their isolation
IGFBP5 is highly expressed in adipocyte precursors and regulates adipogenesis via IGF signaling
FBN1 (fibrillin-1) is a key ECM gene marking mesenchymal/adipocyte precursor identity
MFAP5 (microfibril-associated protein 5) is characteristic of fibroblastic adipocyte progenitors
COL1A1 is a major collagen gene highly expressed in mesenchymal/adipocyte precursor cells
THY1 (CD90) is a surface marker used to identify adipose-derived mesenchymal stem/progenitor cells
DPP4 (CD26) marks a specific adipocyte progenitor subpopulation with high adipogenic potential
Decorin is a proteoglycan characteristic of mesenchymal/fibroblastic adipocyte precursors
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and a definitive B cell lineage marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a canonical B cell surface marker used clinically for B cell identification
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed in B cells as part of MHC class II antigen presentation
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is essential for B cell survival and a specific B cell marker
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR signaling
CD79B is the second component of the BCR signaling complex, specific to B cells
PAX5 is the master transcription factor for B cell lineage commitment and identity
IGKC (immunoglobulin kappa constant) reflects B cell/plasma cell immunoglobulin production
JCHAIN is expressed in antibody-secreting B cells/plasma cells for polymeric Ig assembly
BLK is a Src family kinase specifically expressed in B cells, involved in BCR signaling
TRGC2 (T cell receptor gamma constant 2) is a defining marker of gamma delta T cells
TRDC (T cell receptor delta constant) is the hallmark gene of gamma delta T cells
CD7 is a T/NK cell marker highly expressed on gamma delta T cells
CD8A expression defines the CD8+ subset of gamma delta T cells
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic gamma delta T cells
LEF1 is a TCF/LEF transcription factor marking less differentiated T cell states
TCF7 marks naive/memory-like T cells and is characteristic of tissue-resident gamma delta T cells
XCL1 (lymphotactin) is a chemokine characteristically produced by gamma delta T cells and NK cells
FXYD2 is a tissue-specific ion transport regulator enriched in tissue-resident gamma delta T cells
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor defining tissue-resident lymphocyte identity
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule highly expressed in activated cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic effector protease in CD8+ cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme H is a cytotoxic serine protease characteristic of effector CD8+ T cells
CD8A is the canonical surface marker defining CD8+ T cells
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic granule-mediated killing by CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule membrane protein marking activated cytotoxic lymphocytes
FGFBP2 is highly expressed in terminally differentiated cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic effector CD8+ T cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine abundantly produced by effector CD8+ T cells
TBX21 (T-bet) is the master transcription factor for type 1 cytotoxic T cell differentiation
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells
PECAM1 (CD31) is a canonical endothelial cell adhesion molecule and marker
Von Willebrand factor is produced almost exclusively by endothelial cells and megakaryocytes
CDH5 (VE-cadherin) is the endothelial-specific cadherin essential for vascular integrity
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor specifically expressed on endothelial cells
FABP4 is highly expressed in capillary endothelial cells, especially in adipose tissue
ERG is an ETS transcription factor essential for endothelial cell identity and function
ESAM (endothelial cell-selective adhesion molecule) is specific to endothelial cells
Endomucin is a sialomucin specifically expressed on endothelial cells
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells driving angiogenesis
GZMK (granzyme K) marks immature/less differentiated NK cells compared to GZMB+ mature NK cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor enriched on immature NK cells
CD2 is a surface glycoprotein expressed on NK and T cells, marking NK cell identity
SELL (CD62L) marks less mature NK cells that have not undergone terminal differentiation
NCAM1 (CD56) is the canonical NK cell marker; CD56bright marks immature NK cells
XCL1 is a chemokine characteristically produced by NK cells
XCL2 is a chemokine co-expressed with XCL1 in NK cells
TCF7 expression in NK cells marks an immature/adaptive NK cell state
IL7R marks immature NK cells and NK cell precursors responsive to IL-7
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK cell receptor expressed throughout NK cell development
IL1B is a hallmark pro-inflammatory cytokine of classically activated inflammatory macrophages
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a key chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages for neutrophil recruitment
CD163 is a scavenger receptor and canonical tissue macrophage marker
CD68 is a lysosomal glycoprotein widely used as a pan-macrophage marker
TNF is a major pro-inflammatory cytokine defining the inflammatory macrophage phenotype
PTGS2 (COX-2) is an inducible enzyme in inflammatory macrophages producing prostaglandins
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed by tissue macrophages
FCN1 (ficolin-1) marks monocyte-derived inflammatory macrophages
NLRP3 inflammasome is a hallmark of inflammatory macrophage activation
SOD2 is strongly induced in inflammatory macrophages as part of the oxidative stress response
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical marker of innate lymphoid cells across all ILC subsets
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is highly expressed on ILCs and regulatory lymphocytes
KIT (CD117) is a surface marker expressed on ILC precursors and ILC2/ILC3 subsets
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 and is expressed across ILC subsets
IL7R (CD127) is a key surface marker for identifying ILCs
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated ILCs, particularly ILC2s
IL1RL1 (ST2/IL-33R) is a hallmark receptor of ILC2 cells
AREG (amphiregulin) is a tissue-repair effector molecule produced by ILCs
ID2 is a transcription factor essential for ILC development and identity
IL32 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine highly expressed in ILC1 cells
TBX21 (T-bet) is the master transcription factor defining ILC1 identity
KLRG1 is an inhibitory receptor expressed on mature ILC1 cells
XCL1 is a chemokine characteristically produced by ILC1 and NK cells
ITGB1 (CD29) is expressed on tissue-resident ILC1 cells for tissue retention
ETS1 is a transcription factor important for ILC1 development and function
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor defining tissue-resident ILC1 identity
IL7R (CD127) is a canonical ILC marker expressed on ILC1 cells
SPOCK2 is enriched in ILC1 cells and tissue-resident lymphocytes
IL1RL1 (ST2) is the IL-33 receptor and the most specific marker of ILC2 cells
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 differentiation and function
HPGD (prostaglandin dehydrogenase) is highly expressed in ILC2 cells for prostaglandin metabolism
RORA is a key transcription factor required for ILC2 development
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical surface marker of ILC2 cells
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILC2 cells
PTGDR2 (CRTH2) is a canonical ILC2 surface marker used for their identification
HPGDS produces prostaglandin D2 and is characteristic of ILC2 cells
SLAMF1 is a surface receptor enriched on ILC2 cells
KIT (CD117) is a canonical surface marker of ILC3 cells
CCR6 marks the LTi-like ILC3 subset and is a key ILC3 identifier
IL23R is the IL-23 receptor essential for ILC3 activation and function
IL1R1 is expressed on ILC3 cells enabling responsiveness to IL-1 signaling
LTB (lymphotoxin beta) is produced by ILC3 cells for lymphoid tissue organogenesis
TNFSF11 (RANKL) is expressed by ILC3 cells and involved in lymphoid organogenesis
ICOS is a costimulatory molecule expressed on ILC3 cells
HOXA9 is a transcription factor involved in ILC3 development
DLL1 (Delta-like 1) is a Notch ligand expressed by ILC3 cells
KIT (CD117) marks ILC precursors with multilineage potential
IL7R (CD127) is essential for ILC precursor survival and development
SPINK2 is a marker of hematopoietic progenitors including ILC precursors
SOX4 is a transcription factor important for lymphoid progenitor development
SELL (CD62L) marks circulating ILC precursors before tissue homing
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on ILC precursors
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on ILC precursors and activated ILCs
TCF7 is a transcription factor marking early lymphoid progenitors including ILC precursors
GATA3 is expressed in ILC precursors and required for ILC2 lineage commitment
FABP4 is a fatty acid binding protein defining lipid-associated macrophages (LAMs)
FABP5 is a lipid-handling protein highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages
APOE is a hallmark gene of lipid-associated macrophages involved in lipid metabolism
TREM2 is a defining receptor of lipid-associated macrophages across tissues
SPP1 (osteopontin) is highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages
LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase) is essential for lipid processing in LAMs
CD9 is a tetraspanin that marks lipid-associated macrophages
GPNMB is a glycoprotein highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages
LPL (lipoprotein lipase) is a key lipid metabolism enzyme in LAMs
CD36 is a scavenger receptor for lipid uptake, defining LAM identity
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule abundantly expressed in mature NK cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic protease in mature effector NK cells
FCGR3A (CD16) is the canonical marker of mature CD56dim NK cells
KLRF1 (NKp80) is an activating receptor specific to NK cells
Perforin is essential for NK cell cytotoxicity and marks mature NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is a canonical NK cell receptor
SPON2 is specifically expressed in mature NK cells and involved in innate immunity
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated cytotoxic NK cells
MYOM2 is specifically expressed in mature NK cells
S1PR5 is a sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor marking mature circulating NK cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical surface marker of MAIT cells
RORA is a transcription factor characteristic of MAIT cells
SLC4A10 is one of the most specific markers of MAIT cells
IL7R (CD127) is highly expressed on MAIT cells
RORC (RORγt) is a transcription factor expressed by MAIT cells reflecting their type 17 program
CCR6 is a chemokine receptor characteristic of MAIT cells for mucosal homing
TRGC2 expression reflects the innate-like TCR usage in MAIT cells
GZMK (granzyme K) is highly expressed in MAIT cells reflecting their cytotoxic potential
IL18RAP is expressed on MAIT cells enabling IL-18-driven activation
CXCR6 is a chemokine receptor marking tissue-resident MAIT cells
LYZ (lysozyme) is a canonical myeloid cell marker
S100A9 is a calcium-binding protein highly expressed in myeloid cells
S100A8 forms a heterodimer with S100A9 (calprotectin) in myeloid cells
IL1B is a pro-inflammatory cytokine characteristic of activated myeloid cells
HLA-DRA is an MHC class II gene highly expressed in antigen-presenting myeloid cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein essential for myeloid cell signaling
FCER1G is an Fc receptor signaling chain broadly expressed in myeloid cells
AIF1 (Iba1) is a calcium-binding protein marking myeloid/monocyte lineage cells
FCN1 marks classical monocytes and monocyte-derived myeloid cells
CD14 is a canonical monocyte/myeloid cell surface marker
IL7R (CD127) is highly expressed on naive CD4+ T cells for homeostatic survival
CD40LG (CD154) is expressed on activated CD4+ T cells for B cell help
MAF (c-Maf) is a transcription factor involved in CD4+ T cell differentiation
CD4 is the canonical co-receptor defining CD4+ T helper cells
LEF1 is a transcription factor marking naive T cells
CD28 is a costimulatory receptor highly expressed on naive CD4+ T cells
BCL11B is a transcription factor essential for T cell lineage commitment
FOXP1 is a transcription factor maintaining naive T cell quiescence
CD2 is a T cell surface glycoprotein involved in T cell activation
CD8A is the canonical co-receptor defining CD8+ T cells
CD8B pairs with CD8A to form the CD8 co-receptor on CD8+ T cells
GZMK marks early-activated/naive-like CD8+ T cells before full effector differentiation
CCL5 is produced by CD8+ T cells upon activation
CRTAM is expressed on recently activated CD8+ T cells
CD3E is a component of the TCR complex expressed on all T cells
EOMES is a transcription factor important for CD8+ T cell effector function
RUNX3 is a transcription factor essential for CD8+ T cell lineage identity
SH2D1A (SAP) is involved in T cell signaling and expressed in CD8+ T cells
XCL1 is a chemokine highly produced by NK-like cells
XCL2 is co-expressed with XCL1 in NK-like cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory NK receptor marking NK-like cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein for NK cell activating receptors
FCER1G is an Fc receptor signaling chain expressed in NK-like cells
NCAM1 (CD56) is the canonical NK cell marker
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule protein expressed in NK-like cells
CSF2 (GM-CSF) production distinguishes NK-like cells with innate immune function
CD160 is an activating receptor expressed on NK-like cells
IRF8 is a transcription factor expressed in NK-like cells with innate immune function
S100A9 is one of the most abundant proteins in neutrophils forming calprotectin
S100A8 pairs with S100A9 to form calprotectin, a hallmark neutrophil protein
FCGR2A (CD32) is an Fc receptor expressed on neutrophils for immune complex binding
CSF3R (G-CSF receptor) is essential for neutrophil development and survival
FCAR (CD89) is the IgA Fc receptor specifically expressed on neutrophils
FPR1 is a formyl peptide receptor highly expressed on neutrophils for chemotaxis
SERPINB2 (PAI-2) is highly expressed in activated neutrophils
AQP9 is an aquaporin channel highly expressed in neutrophils
FCN1 is a pattern recognition molecule expressed in neutrophils and monocytes
S100A12 (calgranulin C) is specifically expressed in neutrophils
FCGR3A (CD16) is the defining marker of nonclassical monocytes
LST1 is highly expressed in nonclassical monocytes
CDKN1C is a cell cycle inhibitor specifically marking nonclassical monocytes
LILRA5 is an immunoglobulin-like receptor enriched in nonclassical monocytes
VMO1 is specifically expressed in nonclassical monocytes
HES4 is a Notch target gene enriched in nonclassical monocytes
MS4A7 is a membrane-spanning protein marking nonclassical monocytes
SERPINA1 (alpha-1 antitrypsin) is highly expressed in nonclassical monocytes
LYPD2 is a GPI-anchored protein specifically expressed on nonclassical monocytes
AIF1 (Iba1) is a myeloid marker highly expressed in nonclassical monocytes
LYVE1 is a canonical marker of perivascular macrophages in multiple tissues
MRC1 (CD206) is a mannose receptor marking tissue-resident perivascular macrophages
FOLR2 is a folate receptor marking tissue-resident macrophages with perivascular localization
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed by tissue-resident macrophages
CD163 is a hemoglobin scavenger receptor marking perivascular macrophages
STAB1 (stabilin-1) is a scavenger receptor specific to perivascular/sinusoidal macrophages
SELENOP is highly expressed in tissue-resident perivascular macrophages
F13A1 (coagulation factor XIII) marks tissue-resident macrophages
DAB2 is an endocytic adaptor protein marking tissue-resident macrophages
COLEC12 (collectin-12) is a scavenger receptor on perivascular macrophages
APOD (apolipoprotein D) is highly expressed in preadipocytes and adipose tissue
CXCL14 is a chemokine specifically expressed by preadipocytes in adipose tissue
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of preadipocytes and adipose progenitor cells
CFD (adipsin) is an adipocyte/preadipocyte marker involved in complement activation
CXCL12 (SDF-1) is produced by preadipocytes and stromal cells in adipose tissue
GPC3 (glypican-3) is expressed in preadipocytes and mesenchymal progenitors
WISP2 is a secreted factor that inhibits adipogenesis, marking the preadipocyte state
IGFBP3 regulates IGF signaling in preadipocytes during adipogenesis
MGP (matrix Gla protein) is highly expressed in preadipocytes
LUM (lumican) is an ECM proteoglycan characteristic of preadipocytes
FOXP3 is the master transcription factor defining regulatory T cells
CTLA4 is constitutively expressed on Tregs and essential for their suppressive function
IL2RA (CD25) is a canonical Treg surface marker
TIGIT is an inhibitory receptor highly expressed on Tregs
IKZF2 (Helios) is a transcription factor marking thymus-derived Tregs
BATF is a transcription factor important for Treg effector function
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is constitutively expressed on Tregs
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated Tregs
LAYN (layilin) marks tumor-infiltrating and activated Tregs
CCR8 is a chemokine receptor specifically marking tissue Tregs
ACTA2 (alpha-smooth muscle actin) is the canonical smooth muscle cell marker
TAGLN (SM22/transgelin) is a smooth muscle-specific actin-binding protein
MYH11 (smooth muscle myosin heavy chain) is the most specific smooth muscle marker
RGS5 is a regulator of G-protein signaling marking pericytes and smooth muscle cells
MYL9 (myosin light chain 9) is a smooth muscle contractile protein
NOTCH3 is a receptor specifically expressed in vascular smooth muscle cells
MYLK (myosin light chain kinase) is essential for smooth muscle contraction
PDGFRB marks pericytes and smooth muscle cells in the vasculature
PLN (phospholamban) regulates calcium handling in smooth muscle cells
GZMK marks tissue-resident NK cells with a less cytotoxic, more regulatory phenotype
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is enriched on tissue-resident NK cells
EOMES is a transcription factor marking tissue-resident NK cells
CD69 is a tissue-residency marker on tissue-resident NK cells
CXCR6 is a chemokine receptor marking tissue-resident NK cells
ITGA6 (CD49f) is expressed on tissue-resident NK cells for tissue retention
TIGIT is an inhibitory receptor expressed on tissue-resident NK cells
CCL5 is abundantly produced by tissue-resident NK cells
ZBTB16 (PLZF) is a transcription factor expressed in tissue-resident NK cells
FASLG (FasL) is expressed on tissue-resident NK cells for cytotoxic function
CLEC9A is the most specific marker of cDC1 (type 1 dendritic cells)
IRF8 is the master transcription factor for cDC1 development
BATF3 is a transcription factor essential for cDC1 differentiation
CADM1 is a cell adhesion molecule specifically expressed on cDC1 cells
IDO1 is an immunomodulatory enzyme highly expressed in cDC1 cells
DNASE1L3 is specifically expressed in cDC1 cells for processing of extracellular DNA
WDFY4 is required for cross-presentation and specifically expressed in cDC1
FLT3 is a receptor tyrosine kinase essential for dendritic cell development
CPVL is a carboxypeptidase highly expressed in cDC1 cells
TACSTD2 is a surface antigen enriched on cDC1 cells
VCAN (versican) is highly expressed in classical monocytes
FCN1 is a canonical marker of classical (type 1) monocytes
CD14 is the defining surface marker of classical monocytes
LYZ (lysozyme) is highly expressed in classical monocytes
CD163 is a scavenger receptor expressed on classical monocytes
CSF1R (CD115) is the M-CSF receptor essential for monocyte development
LYVE1 marks a subset of tissue-infiltrating classical monocytes
MRC1 (CD206) is expressed on classical monocytes differentiating toward macrophages
CLEC10A is a C-type lectin receptor on classical monocytes
S100A8 is a calcium-binding protein expressed in classical monocytes
S100A9 is highly expressed in inflammatory type 2 monocytes
S100A8 forms calprotectin with S100A9, marking inflammatory monocytes
VCAN is highly expressed in classical/inflammatory monocytes
FCN1 marks classical monocytes including the type 2 subset
S100A12 is specifically expressed in inflammatory monocytes
RETN (resistin) is expressed in inflammatory monocytes
CD300LB is a surface receptor enriched on type 2 monocytes
CLEC4E (Mincle) is a C-type lectin receptor on inflammatory monocytes
HSPA1A (HSP70) is a stress-response gene highly expressed in type 2 monocytes
THBS1 (thrombospondin-1) is expressed in activated inflammatory monocytes
CCR7 is a chemokine receptor that marks mature/migratory dendritic cells, highly upregulated in cDC2A upon activation and migration to lymph nodes
LAMP3 (DC-LAMP) is a canonical marker of mature dendritic cells, specifically marking activated DCs that have undergone maturation
FSCN1 (Fascin) is an actin-bundling protein specifically upregulated in mature dendritic cells, essential for dendrite formation and antigen presentation
CCL22 is a chemokine produced by mature dendritic cells to attract regulatory T cells, a hallmark of activated cDC2 subsets
CCL17 (TARC) is a Th2-attracting chemokine characteristically produced by cDC2A cells, distinguishing them from cDC2B
IDO1 is an immunoregulatory enzyme highly expressed in tolerogenic/mature DCs, involved in tryptophan catabolism and immune regulation
CD1E is a lipid antigen-presenting molecule expressed on dendritic cells, particularly cDC2 subsets, involved in lipid antigen processing
CD1B is a lipid antigen-presenting molecule characteristic of cDC2 dendritic cells, distinguishing them from other DC subsets
EBI3 encodes a subunit of IL-27 and IL-35, produced by mature dendritic cells to modulate T cell responses
IRF4 is a key transcription factor that drives cDC2 differentiation and identity, essential for the cDC2A lineage
BIRC3 is an NF-κB pathway regulator upregulated in mature DCs, supporting survival during DC activation
FLT3 is a receptor tyrosine kinase critical for DC development and a canonical marker of conventional dendritic cells
SPIB is a transcription factor important for DC development and function, particularly in cDC2 subsets
CLEC10A (CD301) is a C-type lectin receptor that is a canonical surface marker of cDC2B cells, used to distinguish them from cDC2A
FCER1A (high-affinity IgE receptor alpha) is highly expressed on cDC2B cells, marking their inflammatory and allergic response potential
CD1C (BDCA-1) is a canonical marker of conventional type 2 dendritic cells, particularly enriched in the cDC2B subset
IL1R2 is a decoy receptor for IL-1 highly expressed in cDC2B, reflecting their inflammatory regulatory phenotype
FCGR2B (CD32B) is an inhibitory Fc receptor characteristic of cDC2B cells, involved in immune complex handling
SPI1 (PU.1) is a master transcription factor for myeloid cell development, highly expressed in cDC2B cells
FCER1G is the signaling subunit of Fc receptors, highly expressed in cDC2B reflecting their myeloid/inflammatory character
CD86 is a costimulatory molecule on antigen-presenting cells, highly expressed on cDC2B for T cell activation
ITGAX (CD11c) is a canonical dendritic cell integrin marker, broadly used to identify conventional DCs including cDC2B
IRF8 is a transcription factor involved in DC differentiation, expressed in cDC2B cells as part of their developmental program
AREG (amphiregulin) is an EGF family member highly upregulated in inflammatory cDC2B, reflecting tissue repair and inflammatory functions
LYZ (lysozyme) is a myeloid lineage gene highly expressed in cDC2B, reflecting their monocyte-like inflammatory phenotype
